description_long: This bungalow features a single-story structure with a cross-gabled red roof and light wood-paneled exterior walls. The front facade is dominated by large, floor-to-ceiling glass windows and a central door, allowing ample natural light into the main living area. The side elevations display smaller, symmetrically placed windows, while skylights are integrated into the roof for additional daylight. The building sits on a concrete or stone foundation slab, extending outward to form a small patio or walkway. The overall design is modern yet retains a cozy, residential feel, suitable for suburban or neighborhood settings. The roof overhangs slightly, providing some shelter at the entrance but there is no visible porch.
description: A modern, single-story bungalow with a cross-gabled red roof, large front glass windows, and light wood-paneled walls. The structure sits on a stone or concrete slab and features skylights and symmetrical side windows.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with large glass windows and a door, as well as the cross-gabled roof and the stone or concrete slab foundation.
description_view_1: This view highlights the side of the bungalow with two skylights on the roof and two smaller windows on the wall, emphasizing the roof's slope.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays three symmetrically placed windows and the extended length of the building, with the roof's cross-gable visible.
description_view_3: The rear view shows a plain wooden wall with no windows or doors, and the full expanse of the cross-gabled roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with large front glass windows and grouped side windows
